forbiddenClaims: threats, intimidation, or pressure to prioritise the Lendflow loan over essentials (food, rent, utilities)
fabricated legal consequences (fake bailiff, court action, or enforcement-body threat)
a forbearance-option taxonomy, repayment-plan figure, interest-freeze term, or hardship-assessment script presented as authoritative
a templated repayment figure presented as a guaranteed or only option
gating the free-debt-advice route behind disclosure of personal financial detail
punitive language or pressure tactics on the support-agent view
FCA authorisation, regulation, or affiliation with any real debt-advice charity, collections agency, or enforcement body

rubricAnchors.score1: The journey uses threats, intimidation, or fabricated legal consequences, or gates debt advice behind personal disclosure.
rubricAnchors.score3: The journey is broadly supportive but one phrase leans punitive, or debt advice is reachable only after a step of disclosure.
rubricAnchors.score5: The journey is supportive throughout, surfaces legitimate options, reaches free independent debt advice without gating, and never pushes an unaffordable plan.

rubricAnchors.score1: Payment-difficulty-entry, affordability-review, and support-agent-assist share no common supportive-tone model.
rubricAnchors.score3: Some shared tokens across the three screens but the supportive framing drifts between borrower and agent views.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single supportive direction system unifies all three screens, including a consistent debt-advice route and a consistent no-punitive-language treatment on the agent view.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the debt-advice-reachable, options-not-yet-available, review-service-error, or punitive-language-blocked states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved options-not-yet-available or punitive-language-blocked states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all three screens directly, including the four payment-difficulty-entry states, five affordability-review states, and four support-agent-assist states, with the punitive-language-blocked guard made explicit.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing punitive collections or arreals-management page that fabricates legal consequences.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common payment-difficulty patterns but the supportive-tone and no-punitive-agent treatments are visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes the supportive-not-punitive boundary, free debt-advice route, and honest affordability review legible without diluting the support job.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no entry-load-error, review-service-error, or punitive-language-blocked states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of the entry-load-error and review-service-error states.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all three screens, with robust entry-load-error, review-service-error, options-not-yet-available, and punitive-language-blocked states that preserve the supportive framing.

rubricAnchors.score1: A fabricated forbearance taxonomy, repayment-plan figure, interest-freeze term, or hardship script appears anywhere; punitive language is presented as legitimate.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one forbearance option or repayment figure slips through as a concrete number.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, forbearance options and repayment figures surface as templated placeholders or dynamic pulls, and no FCA, charity-affiliation, or enforcement-body claim appears.

adjudicationNotes: Any threat, intimidation, fabricated legal consequence, or pressure to prioritise the loan over essentials is an automatic fail on product-appropriateness and evidence-discipline.
The free-debt-advice route is load-bearing: it must be reachable WITHOUT prior personal-financial disclosure; gating it fails product-appropriateness.
The support-agent view's punitive-language-blocked state exists specifically to forbid punitive framing even on the staff view; weakening it fails accessibility-and-failure-states.
Affordability review must be honest: pushing a borrower into an unaffordable repayment plan, or presenting a templated figure as the only option, fails product-appropriateness.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes constraints (supportive tone, free advice, honest review), not visual identity.
